prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. *"Lo! In this third quarter of the Year of Our Lord Two Thousand and Twenty-Four, the sorcerers of 'ZoomlyCloud Inc.' didst summon forth a great host of 'Recurring Revenues,' which, though invisible to the mortal eye, did swell their coffers by a most bewildering twenty-three percent—yet the scribes murmur that this was but 'flat YoY growth,' a phrase as cryptic as the runes of the Norns."*

2. *"The High Alchemist (or 'CEO,' as they style themselves) did boast of 'churn reduction,' though I swear by Saint Isidor’s quill that no butter nor cheese was involved—nay, ‘twas but the departure of fewer peasant—er, 'customers'—from their digital fiefdom, a miracle wrought by 'AI-driven engagement tools,' which I can only assume are enchanted golem-scribes."*

3. *"Most perplexing was the 'gross margin expansion,' a phenomenon whereby the company’s gold grew heavier whilst their expenditures remained as insubstantial as a bishop’s vow of chastity; the court astrologers (called 'analysts') did nod sagely and speak of 'operational leverage,' though I suspect black magic."*

4. *"Yet woe! For though the 'MRR' (a demon whose name I dare not speak in full) did climb like a siege tower, the 'burn rate' doth still consume their treasury as a dragon doth a maiden—wherefore the board hath decreed a 'strategic pivot to PLG,' a maneuver as mysterious as the Holy Roman Emperor’s foreign policy, but with fewer horses."*
